title: QUIC client-side 1-RTT Key Phase observation verification
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests, fuzzing, BenchmarkDotNet coverage, and SpecTrace render/validation checks.
scope: Verify that the short-header helper preserves and reports the Key Phase bit, that an active handshake-confirmed client runtime records the first observed 1-RTT Key Phase transition by installing the existing internal state, that an establishing runtime remains unchanged before handshake confirmation, and that the slice does not claim successor-key derivation, TLS KeyUpdate support, transfer, retry, or public API widening.
preconditions: The repository builds successfully on net10.0.
REQ-QUIC-CRT-0137 remains a landed prerequisite for the handshake-confirmed active client runtime fixture.
REQ-QUIC-CRT-0144 is the active key-phase requirement in this slice.
A permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite exists under benchmarks/README.md for the 1-RTT Key Phase helper path.
procedure: Run the focused requirement-home tests for helper round-trip coverage, active-runtime first-transition install, establishing-runtime no-install behavior, fuzz coverage for the short-header Key Phase boundary, and the public no-broad-support guard.
Run the permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for the 1-RTT Key Phase helper path.
Render the SpecTrace markdown from the canonical JSON and validate the generated artifacts.
expected_result: The short-header helper preserves the Key Phase bit, the active client runtime installs the first observed 1-RTT Key Phase transition only after handshake confirmation, the establishing runtime remains unchanged before handshake confirmation, and the library does not claim successor-key derivation or public key-update support.

supplemental_sections.heading: Acceptance Criteria
supplemental_sections.content:
- The short-header helper preserves and reports the Key Phase bit.
- The active client runtime records the first observed 1-RTT Key Phase transition by updating the existing internal state.
- The establishing runtime remains unchanged before handshake confirmation.
- The slice does not claim successor-key derivation, TLS KeyUpdate support, transfer, retry, or public API widening.
